sections.heading: CNC Machining in PTFE/Teflon – What is PTFE Plastic?
sections.text: CNC Machining in PTFE/Teflon – What is PTFE Plastic? The mechanical properties allow PTFE CNC machining to be used in the production of Teflon machined parts for broad usages across different industries. What is PTFE plastic or Teflon? Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) is a versatile engineering plastic and synthetic fluoropolymer of tetrafluoroethylene with outstanding performance and properties. Teflon is a brand name of PTFE material. Polytetrafluoroethylene is a fluorocarbon solid, which is a polymer compound composed of carbon and fluorine. PTFE is hydrophobic, and neither water nor aqueous substances will wet it. The most typical application of PTFE is the nonstick coating of pots and ot...

sections.heading: Benefits of PTFE Plastic
sections.text: Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) is a versatile engineering plastic and synthetic fluoropolymer of tetrafluoroethylene with outstanding performance and properties. Teflon is a brand name of PTFE material. Polytetrafluoroethylene is a fluorocarbon solid, which is a polymer compound composed of carbon and fluorine. PTFE is hydrophobic, and neither water nor aqueous substances will wet it. The most typical application of PTFE is the nonstick coating of pots and other cooking utensils, and it is often used in containers and pipes with active and corrosive chemicals. When PTFE is used as a lubricant due to the incredibly low friction, it can reduce friction, wear and machine energy consumption. It...

sections.heading: Properties and Applications of Machined Teflon/PTFE
sections.text: Properties and Applications of Machined Teflon/PTFE PTFE material is mainly used for laying wires such as connecting wires, and coaxial cables in aerospace and computer fields. Machined Teflon has excellent dielectric properties, which is very suitable for connector components at high RF and PCB (printed circuit board) insulation at microwave frequency. Due to its high melting point, it has become a high-performance substitute for polyethylene in low-cost applications. In industrial applications, due to its low friction, PTFE is used in sliding bearings, gears, sliding plates, seals, washers, bushings, and more occasions with sliding parts. Its performance is better than acetal and nylon. I...
